arts.ttl: Amorim: Rashford had to leave as he didn't adapt
arts.sum: Ruben Amorim has said Marcus Rashford had to leave Manchester United because he couldn't get the forward to buy into his ideas.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim does not fear sack even if Manchester United’s slump worsens
arts.sum: Head coach has lost seven league games since his arrival Amorim: ‘I couldn’t get Rashford to see football how I do’ Ruben Amorim does not fear the sack even if Manchester United’s slump worsens under his watch, with the head coach sure of co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe’s support. United’s 2-0 defeat to Crystal Palace on Sunday was a seventh loss in 13 Premier League games under Amorim with the result leaving them in 13th place with a -6 goal difference. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim: I couldn’t get Marcus Rashford to see football the way I do
arts.sum: Rashford has joined Aston Villa on loan Manchester United for the rest of the season after falling out of favour under Amorim.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim says respect was key in his decision to release Ruud van Nistelrooy
arts.sum: The Manchester United boss knows retaining the Dutchman would have been welcomed by many at Old Trafford.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim insists Marcus Rashford left him no option but to axe him from Man Utd
arts.sum: Marcus Rashford has departed Manchester United for Aston Villa on loan after falling completely out of favour under new boss Ruben Amorim who stresses he just couldn't get the striker on the same page

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy recalls Cristiano Ronaldo's rise at Man Utd - "I was lucky"
arts.sum: Cristiano Ronaldo arrived at Old Trafford as a fresh-faced teenager with raw ability while Ruud van Nistelrooy was Manchester United's top dog - though the tables quickly turned

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim admits his Man Utd job is in danger after taking major "risk"
arts.sum: Manchester United have struggled enormously this season and the appointment of Ruben Amorim has done little to arrest their slide down the table while the new manager opted against buying a new striker in January

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy names two Leicester City teenagers on bench against Manchester United
arts.sum: Jeremy Monga and Jake Evans will be on the Leicester City bench at Manchester United as Ruud van Nistelrooy promotes the two talented youngsters into his first team. “The FA Cup is also fantastic to add young players into my squad, a 15-year-old and a 16-year-old,” van Nistelrooy said at his press conference on Thursday morning.

arts.ttl: 'I couldn't' - Ruben Amorim makes Marcus Rashford training claim after Manchester United exit
arts.sum: Marcus Rashford may have played for Man United for the last time after he joined Aston Villa on loan for the rest of the season.

arts.ttl: Greatest 'wind-up' celebrations in Premier League history: Emmanuel Adebayor gets his Arsenal revenge, Jamie Vardy's constant Tottenham jibes and the origin of Neal Maupay's beef with James Maddison
arts.sum: Thirty-three years worth of Premier League football has produced quite the catalogue of 'wind-up' celebrations, but, in truth, we are entering a golden age.
